ops.title: semsearch: chunker language coverage — embed common grammars + unknown-ext line fallback
ops.description: Review findings C1 + I2 + C4 (verified 2026-07-22, deferred from the native-search ship). C1: only the 19 benchmark grammars are go:embed'd; parser-supported languages outside that set (.tsx, .css, .scss, .vue, .svelte, .html) fall back to line chunking where semble AST-chunks them — .tsx is very common (React), so this is the top-priority coverage gap. Binary-size tradeoff (each grammar ~100-600KB). I2: a .gitignore negation (!file.ext) can admit a file whose extension is unknown; the walker includes it but chunk.Chunk drops unknown extensions -> the file silently vanishes, or a single-file repo errors 'no indexable files'. Fix: line-chunk walker-admitted unknown-ext files (force language=''). C4 (low): invalid-UTF-8 replacement is byte-by-byte; Python groups maximal invalid subsequences into one U+FFFD -> Content/offsets differ on files with truncated multibyte tails. Graceful/deterministic today; not a correctness blocker.

ops.text: tsx/css/scss AST-chunked (grammars embedded); walker-admitted unknown-ext files line-chunked not dropped; UTF-8 replacement matches Python maximal-subpart
